records.ety: [AS. wearm; akin to OS., OFries., D., & G. warm, Icel. varmr, Sw. & Dan. varm, Goth. warmjan to warm; probably akin to Lith. virti to cook, boil; or perhaps to Skr. gharma heat, OL. formus warm. <?/<?/<?/, <?/<?/<?/.]

defs: Having heat in a moderate degree; not cold as, warm milk.
quotes: Warm and still is the summer night.

defs: Having a sensation of heat, esp. of gentle heat; glowing.
Subject to heat; having prevalence of heat, or little or no cold weather; as, the warm climate of Egypt.
Fig.: Not cool, indifferent, lukewarm, or the like, in spirit or temper; zealous; ardent; fervent; excited; sprightly; irritable; excitable.
quotes: Mirth, and youth, and warm desire!
Each warm wish springs mutual from the heart.
They say he's warm man and does not care to be mad<?/ mouths at.
I had been none of the warmest of partisans.

defs: Violent; vehement; furious; excited; passionate; as, a warm contest; a warm debate.
quotes: Welcome, daylight; we shall have warm work on't.

defs: Being well off as to property, or in good circumstances; forehanded; rich.
quotes: Warm householders, every one of them.
You shall have a draft upon him, payable at sight: and let me tell you he as warm a man as any within five miles round him.

defs: In children's games, being near the object sought for; hence, being close to the discovery of some person, thing, or fact concealed.
quotes: Here, indeed, young Mr. Dowse was getting “warm,” <?/<?/ children say at blindman's buff.

defs: Having yellow or red for a basis, or in their composition; -- said of colors, and opposed to cold which is of blue and its compounds.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Warmed (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Warming.]

defs: To communicate a moderate degree of heat to; to render warm; to supply or furnish heat to; as, a stove warms an apartment.
quotes: Then shall it [an ash tree] be for a man to burn; for he will take thereof and warm himself.
Enough to warm, but not enough to burn.

defs: To make engaged or earnest; to interest; to engage; to excite ardor or zeal; to enliven.
quotes: I formerly warmed my head with reading controversial writings.
Bright hopes, that erst bosom warmed.

defs: To become warm, or moderately heated; as, the earth soon warms in a clear day summer.
quotes: There shall not be a coal to warm at.

defs: To become ardent or animated; as, the speake<?/ warms as he proceeds.
The act of warming, or the state of being warmed; a warming; a heating.
